description_long: This is a traditional Japanese katana displayed on a dark wooden stand. The katana features a long, gently curved, single-edged blade with a visible hamon (temper line) and a circular guard (tsuba) separating the blade from the handle. The handle is wrapped in a patterned material, likely ray skin and cord, and ends in a metal pommel. Below the katana, a matching scabbard (saya) rests on the stand, decorated with subtle patterns and a cord wrap near the opening. The wooden stand has a polished, glossy finish and supports both the blade and the scabbard securely. The overall presentation suggests a ceremonial or decorative purpose, emphasizing both function and artistry.
description: A traditional Japanese katana with a curved blade and ornate handle, displayed on a polished wooden stand with a matching scabbard.
description_view_0: The katana and scabbard are seen from the side, with the blade and handle aligned, showing the thickness and layered arrangement on the stand.
description_view_1: A full side view reveals the entire length of the katana, the detailed handle wrap, the circular guard, and the decorative scabbard resting below.
description_view_2: The opposite side view highlights the alignment of the blade and scabbard, with the handle and scabbard ends visible in profile.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the straight alignment of the katana and scabbard on the stand, with the handle and scabbard cord details visible.
attribute_handle_design: wrapped, cylindrical handle with cord and ray skin, metal pommel
attribute_ornamentation: subtle patterns on scabbard and handle wrap
